Thus, to ensure semantic equivalence: (1) PyTorch test case must use torch.linalg.eigh with UPLO='U' to match original 'upper=True'; (2) TensorFlow test case must drop 'eigenvectors' (invalid param) and retain default behavior (returns both); (3) both APIs require symmetric/self-adjoint input, so input generation logic must ensure symmetry — but that's handled at test runtime, not in the case spec. Hence, repair involves updating APIs and aligning parameters: PyTorch → torch.linalg.eigh with UPLO='U', TensorFlow → tf.linalg.eigh without 'eigenvectors', and remove 'upper' from TF case since it's unsupported and irrelevant.
